title: GitHub Issues intake + done/blocked notifications (phone-facing backlog)
description: Bridge to the remote-operation goal: tickets arranged from a phone, outcomes reported back to it. Intake: a sync script pulls GitHub Issues labeled adw from the repo into prd.json stories (issue body carries description + acceptance criteria; labels carry type/priority), so the GitHub mobile app becomes the write-capable taskboard. Outbound: when a workflow finishes a ticket, it opens/updates a PR for the adw/<id> branch and comments the outcome (done/blocked + reason) on the source issue, so GitHub push notifications reach the phone. Requires the existing git-credential token; no new services.
acceptance_criteria: workflows/sync_issues.py converts open GitHub Issues labeled adw into valid prd.json stories with a deterministic id mapping and never duplicates existing stories
Malformed issues are skipped with a printed reason, never written into prd.json
On ticket completion the workflow opens (or updates) a PR for adw/<id> and comments done/blocked + reason on the source issue
All GitHub calls go through the REST API with the locally stored credential; the scripts degrade with a clear error when offline
Unit tests cover the issue-to-story mapping and skip logic with fixture payloads (no live API in tests)
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green
